frameworks.name: WIEP
frameworks.fullName: Western Australian Institute of Education Planning
frameworks.description: The standard Western Australian lesson planning format. Four-phase structure that is required in WA schools and SCSA-aligned.
frameworks.whenToUse: Default for all WA lesson planning. Required format for TRBWA documentation.

structure.description: State the specific learning intention in student-friendly language. Make the 'why' clear.
How you teach it — explicit instruction, modelling, guided practice, independent tasks.
How you'll know they've learned — exit tickets, check for understanding, observations.
Activities, resources, timing, differentiation notes.

frameworks.name: 5E Model
frameworks.description: Five-phase inquiry model originally from science education. Widely used for HASS and Science, effective for building conceptual understanding.
frameworks.whenToUse: Science, HASS, any inquiry-based unit. Best for Years 3-6 where students have prior knowledge to build on.

structure.description: Hook students — question, mystery, demonstration. Activates prior knowledge.
Hands-on investigation. Students discover concepts through doing, not listening.
Explicit teaching of new concepts. Connect to exploration findings.
Apply understanding to new situations. Extend and deepen learning.
Check for understanding — formative assessment, self-evaluation, exit ticket.

frameworks.name: Gradual Release
frameworks.tagline: I Do → We Do → You Do
frameworks.description: The foundational explicit teaching model. Teacher moves from full responsibility (I Do) to shared (We Do) to independent (You Do).
frameworks.whenToUse: New concepts, Years 1-4, literacy and numeracy foundations. Best for explicit teaching of skills and strategies.

structure.description: Teacher models, thinks aloud. Makes expert thinking visible. No student output yet.
Guided practice together. Teacher and students work collaboratively. Scaffolding provided.
Independent practice. Students work alone while teacher monitors and assesses.

frameworks.name: Direct Instruction
frameworks.description: Highly structured, teacher-led instruction with clear objectives, scripted teaching, and rapid-fire practice. Based on Siegfried Engelmann's research.
frameworks.whenToUse: New concepts that need clear explanation, foundational skills that require drill and practice, years F-3 for literacy and numeracy basics.

structure.description: State objective clearly. Review prerequisite skills.
New content presented in small steps. Clear explanations, modelling, examples.
Controlled practice with immediate feedback. Signal reinforcement.
Solo practice with reducing support. Monitor and correct errors immediately.
Review what was learned, preview next lesson.

frameworks.name: Inquiry-Based
frameworks.description: Students drive learning through questions, investigation, and discovery. Teacher acts as facilitator and guide rather than primary information source.
frameworks.whenToUse: Open-ended projects, HASS and Science, Years 4-6, when students have sufficient prior knowledge to frame meaningful questions.

structure.description: Students generate questions from a stimulus. Teacher guides selection of inquiry question.
Students research, collect data, experiment. Teacher facilitates and provides resources.
Students make meaning from data. Develop explanations, create products.
Share findings. Debate, critique, refine understanding.
Students reflect on learning process and product. Self and peer assessment.
